articles.title: Stand Up for Research, Innovation, and Education
articles.subtitle: Right now, MIT alumni and friends are voicing their support for: America’s scientific and technological leadership Merit-based admissions and affordable education Advances that increase US health, security, and prosperity Our community is s
articles.author: the MIT Alumni Association
articles.summary: Right now, MIT alumni and friends are voicing their support for: America’s scientific and technological leadership Merit-based admissions and affordable education Advances that increase US health, security, and prosperity Our community is standing up for MIT and its mission to serve the nation and the world. And we need you to join us at this…
body: Right now, MIT alumni and friends are voicing their support for:
Launching next week on T-Mobile's network, the cell plan takes a nuclear approach to online safety.
Musk kept his cool, and OpenAI’s lawyer bulldozed him with piercing questions about his motivations for suing the company.
The country wants to become a global leader in brain implants. Strong government support is expected to help accelerate that process.
Subquadratic has now shared more details about its new model. But some are still skeptical.
Discover special offers, top stories, upcoming events, and more.

paragraphTranslations: 目前，麻省理工学院的校友和朋友们正在表达他们对以下内容的支持：
下周在T-Mobile的网络上推出的这个手机计划，对网络安全采取了一种核方法。
马斯克保持冷静，而OpenAI的律师用尖锐的问题对他进行了逼问，询问他起诉公司的原因。
这个国家希望成为脑植入物的全球领导者。预计强有力的政府支持将有助于加速这一进程。
Subquadratic现在分享了更多关于其新模型的细节。但仍有人持怀疑态度。

paragraphs: MIT alumni and friends are supporting research, innovation, and education. T-Mobile will launch a new cell plan next week that focuses on online safety. Elon Musk faced tough questions from OpenAI's lawyer about his reasons for suing the company. A country is aiming to be a global leader in brain implants, with government support expected to speed up the process. Subquadratic has shared more details about its new model, but some are still skeptical. Technology Review offers special offers, top stories, upcoming events, and more.

articles.title: Reinventing the zipper
articles.subtitle: With an adaptable fastener designed at CSAIL, pitching a tent or adjusting the cast for a broken bone could be almost as easy as zipping your coat. The researchers, led by associate professor Stefanie Mueller, were inspired by an abandoned
articles.author: Alex Shipps
articles.summary: With an adaptable fastener designed at CSAIL, pitching a tent or adjusting the cast for a broken bone could be almost as easy as zipping your coat. The researchers, led by associate professor Stefanie Mueller, were inspired by an abandoned prototype for a three-sided zipper that William Freeman, PhD ’92 (now an MIT professor), patented…
body: A 40-year-old patent inspired a three-sided fastener that lets objects take shape in instants.
With an adaptable fastener designed at CSAIL, pitching a tent or adjusting the cast for a broken bone could be almost as easy as zipping your coat.

The researchers, led by associate professor Stefanie Mueller, were inspired by an abandoned prototype for a three-sided zipper that William Freeman, PhD ’92 (now an MIT professor), patented in the mid-1980s. With software they created, people can design customized versions of this fastener to be 3D-printed in plastic. Its three flexible “arms” can then form different types of rigid objects when zipped together.
Users can select the strips’ length and the direction and angle at which they’ll bend. They can also choose whether the zipped-up device will appear straight, bent, coiled, or twisted.

A tent making use of the zipper takes just 80 seconds to set up. A wrist cast that incorporates it can be tightened and loosened as desired. A motor can even be added so that, for example, a robot’s leg heights could change with the push of a button.

“A regular zipper is great for closing up flat objects, like a jacket, but Freeman ideated something more dynamic,” says MIT postdoc Jiaji Li, a lead author on a paper presenting the project. “We’ve developed a process that builds objects you can rapidly shift from flexible to rigid, and you can be confident they’ll work in the real world.”
Launching next week on T-Mobile's network, the cell plan takes a nuclear approach to online safety.
Musk kept his cool, and OpenAI’s lawyer bulldozed him with piercing questions about his motivations for suing the company.
The country wants to become a global leader in brain implants. Strong government support is expected to help accelerate that process.
Subquadratic has now shared more details about its new model. But some are still skeptical.
Discover special offers, top stories, upcoming events, and more.

We’re having trouble saving your preferences. Try refreshing this page and updating them one more time. If you continue to get this message, reach out to us at customer-service@technologyreview.com with a list of newsletters you’d like to receive.
keyPoints: A 40-year-old patent inspired a three-sided fastener that lets objects take shape in instants.
With an adaptable fastener designed at CSAIL, pitching a tent or adjusting the cast for a broken bone could be almost as easy as zipping your coat.

The researchers, led by associate professor Stefanie Mueller, were inspired by an abandoned prototype for a three-sided zipper that William Freeman, PhD ’92 (now an MIT professor), patented in the mid-1980s. With software they created, people can design customized versions of this fastener to be 3D-printed in plastic. Its three flexible “arms” can then form different types of rigid objects when zipped together.
paragraphTranslations: 一个40年的专利启发了一个三面扣件，可以让物体瞬间成形。
在CSAIL设计的可适应扣件，搭帐篷或调整断骨的石膏几乎可以像拉上你的外套一样简单。
由副教授Stefanie Mueller领导的研究人员，受到了William Freeman博士（现为麻省理工学院教授）在1980年代中期专利的一个废弃的三面拉链原型的启发。他们用自己创建的软件，人们可以设计这种扣件的定制版本，用塑料3D打印出来。它的三个灵活的“臂”在拉上时可以形成不同类型的刚性物体。
用户可以选择条带的长度以及它们弯曲的方向和角度。他们还可以选择拉上后的设备看起来是直的、弯曲的、盘绕的还是扭曲的。
使用拉链的帐篷搭建只需80秒。一个融入它的手腕石膏可以根据需要收紧和放松。甚至可以添加一个电机，例如，机器人的腿高可以通过按一下按钮来改变。
“普通的拉链非常适合封闭平坦的物体，比如夹克，但Freeman构想了更动态的东西，”麻省理工学院的博士后Jiaji Li说，他是一篇介绍该项目的论文的主要作者。“我们已经开发了一种过程，可以快速将物体从灵活转变为刚性，并且你可以确信它们将在现实世界中工作。”
下周在T-Mobile的网络上推出的这个手机计划，采取了一种核方法来保护在线安全。
马斯克保持冷静，而OpenAI的律师用尖锐的问题对他进行猛烈抨击，询问他起诉公司的动力。
这个国家希望成为大脑植入物的全球领导者。预计强有力的政府支持将有助于加速这一进程。
Subquadratic现在已经分享了更多关于其新模型的细节。但仍有人持怀疑态度。

vocabulary.example: We can offer customized services to meet your needs.

vocabulary.example: The model is made from 3D-printed parts.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 这个模型是由3D打印部件制成的。

vocabulary.example: The schedule is flexible, you can choose any day.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 日程是灵活的，你可以选择任何一天。

vocabulary.example: The rules are too rigid and need to be changed.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 规则太死板，需要改变。

vocabulary.example: The company is known for its technological innovation.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 这家公司以其技术创新而闻名。

paragraphs: Researchers at CSAIL have created a three-sided fastener inspired by a 40-year-old patent, which can be used to quickly assemble various rigid objects. The adaptable fastener can be customized and 3D-printed in plastic, with its three flexible arms forming different shapes when zipped together. Users can choose the length, bending direction, and angle of the strips, and the final shape of the zipped object, whether straight, bent, coiled, or twisted. This innovation can significantly reduce the time needed for tasks like pitching a tent, which takes only 80 seconds, or adjusting a wrist cast.
paragraphTranslations: 麻省理工学院计算机科学与人工智能实验室的研究人员根据一个40年前的专利，发明了一种三面扣件，可以快速组装各种刚性物体。这种可调节的扣件可以定制并用塑料3D打印，其三个灵活的臂在拉链拉紧时可以形成不同的形状。用户可以选择条带的长度、弯曲方向和角度，以及拉链物体的最终形状，无论是直的、弯曲的、盘绕的还是扭曲的。这项创新可以大幅减少搭建帐篷等任务所需的时间，搭建帐篷只需80秒，或者调整手腕托架。

The adaptable fastener can be customized and 3D-printed in plastic, with its three flexible arms forming different shapes when zipped together. Users can choose the length, bending direction, and angle of the strips, and the final shape of the zipped object, whether straight, bent, coiled, or twisted. This innovation can significantly reduce the time needed for tasks like pitching a tent, which takes only 80 seconds, or adjusting a wrist cast.
